perturbation.original_text: Section 4.4. Termination of Advisory Agreement. This Agreement shall terminate automatically without notice and immediately (a) if MS Capital Partners Adviser Inc. or another affiliate of Licensor is no longer acting as the investment adviser (any such entity, the "Advisor") to Licensee under the Investment Advisory Agreement, dated as of [·], 201[9] (as the same may 2 Source: MORGAN STANLEY DIRECT LENDING FUND, 10-12G/A, 11/19/2019 be amended, modified or otherwise restated, the "Investment Advisory Agreement"), or a similar agreement, or (b) the Advisor is no longer an affiliate of Licensor. Further, Licensor may terminate this Agreement, effective upon written notice, at any time after 30 days from the date that Licensee notifies Licensor that the Investment Advisory Agreement has terminated or is not being renewed. The term "affiliate" as used herein shall have the meaning given to such term in the Investment Advisory Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 4.4. Termination of Advisory Agreement. This Agreement shall terminate automatically without notice and immediately (a) if MS Capital Partners Adviser Inc. or another affiliate of Licensor is no longer acting as the investment adviser (any such entity, the "Advisor") to Licensee under the Investment Advisory Agreement, dated as of [·], 201[9] (as the same may 2 Source: MORGAN STANLEY DIRECT LENDING FUND, 10-12G/A, 11/19/2019 be amended, modified or otherwise restated, the "Investment Advisory Agreement"), or a similar agreement, or (b) the Advisor is no longer an affiliate of Licensor. Further, Licensor may terminate this Agreement, effective upon written notice, at any time after 30 days from the date that Licensee notifies Licensor that the Investment Advisory Agreement has terminated or is not being renewed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'The term "affiliate" as used herein shall have the meaning given to such term in the Investment Advisory Agreement.', the agreement fails to specify how 'affiliate' is defined, creating ambiguity. This affects Section 7.1 where indemnity is extended to 'Licensor and its affiliates', but it's now unclear who precisely qualifies as an affiliate, creating an in-text contradiction as the agreement still refers to affiliates but lacks a concrete definition for the term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 7.1. Indemnity by Licensee. Licensee will defend at its expense, indemnify and hold harmless Licensor and its affiliates and its and their respective directors, officers, employees, shareholders, investors, agents and representatives from any losses, liabilities, obligations, damages, awards, settlements, judgments, fees, costs or expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees and costs of suit) arising out of or relating to any third-party Action against any of them that arises out of or relates to (i) any breach by Licensee of this Agreement or its warranties, representations, covenants and undertakings hereunder, (ii) Licensee's operation of the Licensee Business or (iii) any claim that Licensee's use of the Brand, other than as explicitly authorized by this Agreement, Infringes the rights of a third party.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the sentence defining 'affiliate' creates a significant ambiguity in Section 7.1, where 'Licensor and its affiliates' are indemnified. Without a clear definition, it is unclear who qualifies as an 'affiliate,' rendering the indemnity provision uncertain and potentially unenforceable, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Section 3.2. Prior Written Approval. Prior to using the Brand in any manner, Licensee shall submit all proposed uses to Licensor for prior written approval.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'Prior to using the Brand in any manner, Licensee shall submit all proposed uses to Licensor for prior written approval.', there is no clear procedure to ensure the 'Quality Control' in Section 3.1. Thus, there is a contradiction within the text, as this section mentions that, 'Licensee's Permitted Activity shall be in a manner consistent with Licensor's high standards of and reputation for quality, and in accordance with good trademark practice wherever any of the same are used. Licensee shall not take any action that could reasonably be expected to harm the Brand or the goodwill associated therewith'. However, with no prior written approval of uses of the brand, there is a clear ambiguity on how is going to be possible to ensure the brand standards, and ensure the consistency of the Licensor's high standards of quality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3.1. Quality Control. Licensee's Permitted Activity shall be in a manner consistent with Licensor's high standards of and reputation for quality, and in accordance with good trademark practice wherever any of the same are used. Licensee shall not take any action that could reasonably be expected to harm the Brand or the goodwill associated therewith. Licensee shall use with the Brand any applicable trademark notices as may be requested by Licensor or required under applicable laws, regulations, stock exchange and other rules ("Laws") and reputable industry practice.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3.2 mandated prior written approval from the Licensor for any use of the Brand by the Licensee. The changed text removes this requirement entirely. Section 3.1, titled 'Quality Control,' explicitly states that 'Licensee's Permitted Activity shall be in a manner consistent with Licensor's high standards of and reputation for quality...'. Removing the prior approval process contradicts this quality control provision because it eliminates the mechanism by which the Licensor can ensure the Licensee's use of the brand adheres to those standards, creating a clear and direct conflict within the contract. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 9.5. Waiver of Jury Trial. EACH PARTY HEREBY IRREVOCABLY AND UNCONDITIONALLY WAIVES TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, ANY RIGHT SUCH PARTY MAY HAVE TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN RESPECT TO ANY ACTION RELATING TO OR ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT. LICENSEE AGREES THAT LICENSOR WOULD BE IRREPARABLY HARMED BY ANY BREACH OF THIS AGREEMENT BY LICENSEE THAT HARMS THE BRAND, AND THAT LICENSOR MAY (IN ADDITION TO ITS OTHER RIGHTS AND REMEDIES HEREIN) SEEK TEMPORARY, PRELIMINARY OR PERMANENT INJUNCTIVE RELIEF (INCLUDING SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE) TO ENJOIN OR PREVENT ANY SUCH BREACH, WITHOUT POSTING BOND OR OTHER SECURITY.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 9.5. Waiver of Jury Trial. EACH PARTY HEREBY IRREVOCABLY AND UNCONDITIONALLY WAIVES TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, ANY RIGHT SUCH PARTY MAY HAVE TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN RESPECT TO ANY ACTION RELATING TO OR ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the sentence 'LICENSEE AGREES THAT LICENSOR WOULD BE IRREPARABLY HARMED BY ANY BREACH OF THIS AGREEMENT BY LICENSEE THAT HARMS THE BRAND, AND THAT LICENSOR MAY (IN ADDITION TO ITS OTHER RIGHTS AND REMEDIES HEREIN) SEEK TEMPORARY, PRELIMINARY OR PERMANENT INJUNCTIVE RELIEF (INCLUDING SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE) TO ENJOIN OR PREVENT ANY SUCH BREACH, WITHOUT POSTING BOND OR OTHER SECURITY.' contradicts the intention and enforcement of Section 4.2. Since Licensor has 'the right to terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice for any reason, including if the usage of the Brand is not in compliance with the standards and policies'. Now without the sentence, the agreement presents a possible ambiguity as to whether Licensor can easily obtain injunctive relief for brand harm, and if immediate termination is a sufficient remedy, causing an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 4.2. Termination for Convenience. Licensor reserves the right to terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice for any reason, including if the usage of the Brand is not in compliance with the standards and policies.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the sentence regarding irreparable harm and injunctive relief weakens the Licensor's position to protect the brand as stated in Section 4.2, which allows for immediate termination if the usage of the Brand is not in compliance with the standards and policies. Without the ability to easily seek injunctive relief, the 'Termination for Convenience' clause in Section 4.2 might not be a sufficient remedy for brand damage, thus creating an in-text contradiction, which is an omission that contradicts the obligations or constraints stated in other sections, specifically, Section 4.2.
